questions.question: Which of the following best states a theme of the excerpt?

responses.content: A. It is difficult to perceive the big picture with the people closest to you.

questions.question: Based on Perseus's actions in paragraphs 46 through 47, the reader can conclude that—

responses.content: A. Athene will be pleased with the resolution of the plot's conflict.

questions.question: Look at paragraph 10. What is the definition of the word 'protocol' as it is used in the paragraph? Use context clues to determine your answer.

responses.content: The death rate was so high in hatshepsut family because it was more common for people to die and doctors did not use science as much

questions.question: Read paragraph 11. What does the text reveal about Hatshepsut?

responses.content: The definition of the word protocol is the rules and procedures that they follow ancient Egyptian palaces

questions.question: Look at paragraph 14. What confused historians about how Hatshepsut wrote about herself?

responses.content: When Hatshepsut reigned the Egyptian it was a time of peace and prosperity but when the two earlier queens ruled it was political problems

questions.question: Which of the following inferences is best supported by the excerpt?

responses.content: B. Hatshepsut, like many rulers, was a pharaoh first and her maternal duties came second.

questions.question: Which sentence from the excerpt most strongly supports the correct answer to Question 1?

responses.content: A. “At first, little Tuthmosis III was considered the pharaoh, and Hatshepsut just his second-in-command.”
